macro_segments.title: Shomer Aveidah: Classification as Shomer Chinam or Shomer Sachar

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the debate: Rabba vs. Rav Yosef on shomer aveidah
Rabba's view: shomer aveidah is like shomer chinam (no financial benefit)
Rav Yosef's first explanation: hana'ah from exemption of tzedakah obligation
Principle of osek bemitzvah patur min hamitzvah and its limitations
Rav Yosef's second explanation: forced obligation by Torah
Pnei Yehoshua's interpretation: reward for mitzvah as hana'ah

macro_segments.title: Rav Yosef's Challenge to Rabba: Tosefta on Returning Lost Objects

micro_segments.title: Rav Yosef's proof from Tosefta: liability if returned to place where owner will see it
Distinction between morning return and afternoon return
Yam Shel Shlomo on presumptions and individual circumstances

macro_segments.title: Rabba's Defense and Counter-Challenge: Tosefta on Animals and Shmira

micro_segments.title: New Tosefta challenge: shomer aveidah always liable until return to reshut
Rabba concedes regarding animals due to higher shmira requirement
Distinction between animals and inanimate objects in liability

macro_segments.title: Torah's Multiple Phrasings of Hashavat Aveidah and De'at Ba'alim

micro_segments.title: Derivation from Torah: hashev alone implies house only; hashev tashivem extends to garden and ruins
Rabba's reading: unenclosed properties included; Torah teaches multiple return methods
Rav Yosef: enclosed properties required; Rabbi Elazar: no need for de'at ba'alim

macro_segments.title: Shomer Aveidah and To'en Ta'anat Ganav: Abaye's Challenge

micro_segments.title: Abaye challenges Rav Yosef: Rabbi Yochanan says to'en ta'anat ganav pays kefel
Explanation of to'en ta'anat ganav: false claim by shomer to escape liability
Rav Yosef's response: case involves listim mezuyan (armed robbers)
Distinction between ganav and gazlan based on secrecy, not force

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Counter-Challenge: Shomer Sachar Never Pays Kefel

micro_segments.title: Baraita: shomer sachar never pays kefel; therefore no case of ganav applies
Rav Yosef's refined response: listim mezuyan is narrow exception to kefel rule

macro_segments.title: Socher (Renter) Case: Integration with Machloket on Liability Levels

micro_segments.title: Case: renter chooses to pay immediately instead of taking oath
If thief found later, pays kefel to renter (who acquired rights by paying)
Assumption: socher is like noshea sachar (Rabbi Yehuda) or like shomer chinam (Rabbi Meir)
Rabbi Zeira's resolution: case involves false claim of listim mezuyan

macro_segments.title: Rashi's Admission of Uncertainty and Conceptual Issues

micro_segments.title: Rashi admits not knowing source of a teaching; importance of intellectual honesty
Conceptual question: is to'en ta'anat ganav paying kefel due to transformation into ganav or special shomer law

macro_segments.title: Shoel (Borrower) Exemption: Ba'alav Imo and Two Sovereigns

micro_segments.title: Exemption when owner is present during use (ba'alav imo)
Ralbag's explanation: two sovereigns cannot rule over one object simultaneously

macro_segments.title: Animal Falling into Garden: Benefit vs. Damage and Negligence

micro_segments.title: If animal falls into garden and benefits from cushioning, pays back benefit
Rav: if animal ate vegetables, no liability (hayah lah shelo tochal)
Distinction between animal getting damaged vs. animal causing damage

topical_tags.term: osek bemitzvah patur min hamitzvah (one engaged in mitzvah exempt from other mitzvot)
to'en ta'anat ganav (false claim of theft by watchman)
ganav vs. gazlan (thief vs. robber; secrecy vs. force)
Rabbi Meir vs. Rabbi Yehuda (on socher liability)
ba'alav imo (owner is present during use)
hayah lah shelo tochal (animal should not have eaten)
